items.title: 'Whenever biodiversity decays, you decay': French biologist Marc-André Selosse
items.description: A leading environmentalist is warning that the destruction of the world's biodiversity is one of the defining challenges of our time – and one that deserves far greater public attention. Marc-André Selosse, a professor at the French National Museum of Natural History and the University of Gdańsk, and author of the new book "De la biodiversité comme un humanisme" (Biodiversity as a Form of Humanism), argues that nature should be protected not only for its own sake, but because it is essential to humanity's future. He has also criticised politicians, pointing out that biodiversity accounted for just one percent of the debate during France's 2022 presidential election campaign. He spoke to us on Perspective.

items.title: The battle before the 2027 ballots, By Dakuku Peterside
items.description: By the time Nigerians file into polling units in 2027, the most consequential battle for the nation’s democracy may already have been fought — and perhaps won or lost. Elections are merely democracy’s public ceremony, the visible culmination of political choices shaped long before the first ballot is cast. The real test of democratic health […] The post The battle before the 2027 ballots, By Dakuku Peterside appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Yemen government says its forces attacked Sanaa airport
items.description: Gov't says its forces struck Sanaa airport to prevent an Iranian aircraft from landing there.

items.title: Middle East War: Iran claims strikes on US bases in multiple countries
items.description: 🔎 Iran's Revolutionary Guards on Monday said they had hit US military bases in countries across the Middle East in retaliation for American attacks on targets across Iran. Both US and Iran claim control over the Strait of Hormuz. In a telephone interview with Fox News on Monday, President Donald Trump said that the US will "probably become the guardian of the strait," adding that Washington should be "reimbursed" for the same. #Iran #US #StraitofHormuz #MiddleEast

items.title: Unbreakable Albiceleste propel Lionel Messi's final World Cup mission
items.description: No team had ever recovered from a 2-0 deficit with so little time remaining in a World Cup match, but Argentina refused to let Messi's tournament story come to an end. Leer más

items.title: What's behind Germany's World Cup woes?
items.description: Germany have endured three straight disappointing World Cups. Experts say the reasons why are connected to football, but also to German society.

items.title: Infantino opens door to 64-team World Cup
items.description: Plans for a 64-team men's world cup are set to be picked up and assessed in detail after the 2026 tournament with Fifa boss Gianni Infantino saying football needs to be "for the whole world".

items.title: Thai zoo celebrates famous pygmy hippo's second birthday
items.description: In her enclosure at Khao Kheow Open Zoo in Chon Buri, a province east of Bangkok in Thailand, the 2-year-old Moo Deng approached the cake where her mother Jona had already begun digging in

items.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
items.description: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

items.title: Iran war: Trump reinstates US blockade of Strait of Hormuz
items.description: President Donald Trump says the US will restart its naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, targeting Iranian ships and those doing business with Iran. Follow DW for the latest.

items.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
items.description: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

items.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
items.description: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

items.title: FIFA World Cup England vs Argentina: What to know ahead of second semifinal
items.description: Argentina and England meet at the World Cup for the sixth time, facing off in Wednesday's semifinal in Atlanta.

items.title: Renewed US-Iran war is hitting Gulf countries hard
items.description: After US strikes on Iran, Iran has targeted US allies in the Middle East. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

items.title: South African president welcomed by Macron at start of visit to France
items.description: Cyril Ramaphosa will also co-chair a UNESCO meeting on education and take part in the 110th anniversary of the Battle of Delville Wood.

items.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup semi-final place after Belgium win
items.description: Spain celebrated a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium on 10 July as fans filled Madrid to cheer La Roja into the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

items.title: Dollar mostly flat amid escalating US-Iran tension, yen slides on pension doubts
Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. Leer más

items.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
items.description: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

items.title: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
items.description: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

items.title: England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final
items.description: The Albiceleste and their talisman Lionel Messi must defeat England in Atlanta to keep alive their hopes of becoming the first team to win back-to-back World Cups since Brazil in 1962. Leer más

items.title: U.S. hits Iran as Gulf states targeted in flare-up over Hormuz
items.description: Iran responded to the latest U.S. attacks by targeting Gulf nations, with the powerful Revolutionary Guards announcing new strikes on Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait, according to state media reports

items.title: Heavy floods submerge roads, vehicles in northern China
items.description: Authorities warned the storm would dump torrential rain across the Provinces of Jilin, Liaoning, Hebei, Shandong, Jiangsu and Anhui, ‌exacerbating flood risks in areas that had already been soaked by earlier ​downpours

items.title: Some women seeking sperm donation are being coerced into sex
items.description: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: UK counter-terrorism police now leading Widdecombe murder investigation
items.description: The counter-terrorism unit has taken charge of the case due to "new information and evidence" on the suspected murder of the former minister, the UK Home Secretary says.

items.title: Can ageless Lionel Messi keep delivering for Argentina at World Cup?
items.description: Aside from their diminutive genius, Argentina's most potent weapon is their remarkable camaraderie. The defending champions are now unbeaten in 11 World Cup games and the nation is dreaming of a fourth world title. Leer más

items.title: Argentina to repay US$4 billion, defying critics who doubted tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
items.description: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

items.title: Nigeria’s eroding Orimedu coast threatens homes and livelihoods
items.description: This coastline along Orimedu in Nigeria is facing land degradation. The people here are seeing the destruction of their homes as well as the loss of livelihoods.

items.title: Barbados leader rejects claim that ex-colonies should repay Britain
items.description: Barbados Prime Minister Mia Mottley called "asinine" a suggestion by a British lawmaker that Britain's former colonies should repay it for its historic investment in them.

items.title: A versatile actor whose roles went far beyond Jurassic Park
items.description: The actor, who has died aged 78, built up a long string of screen credits over a five-decade career.

items.title: Greyhound industry reaches SA deadline after abuse revelations
items.description: A final report on greyhound industry compliance with improved standards following revelations of widespread abuse is expected to be handed to the SA government in coming months.

items.title: At least 27 killed as fire breaks out at pub in Bangkok
items.description: Firefighters took about half an hour to bring the fire under control; cause of the fire is unknown and under investigation

items.title: South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
items.description: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

items.title: Heavy rains and flash floods in Bangladesh leave 51 dead
items.description: Thousands have lost their homes as flash floods and landslides have hit large parts of the country.

items.title: ‘La cuarta estrella’: Argentina's players embrace fans' new World Cup anthem
items.description: 'La cuarta estrella' has swept through the stands before being adopted by the players themselves after their dramatic victory over Egypt, rekindling memories of Qatar 2022’s 'Muchachos.' Leer más

items.title: De Beers to pause work at S.Africa's largest diamond mine
items.description: De Beers announced Monday it will pause production at South Africa's largest diamond mine for two years to reduce costs while trading conditions remained tough

items.title: Oil prices spike on fresh U.S.-Iran attacks, tech hammers on stocks again
items.description: U.S. military launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Summer soundtrack: Charli XCX and the return of The Strokes
items.description: On today's Arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim brings us a roundup of upcoming album releases to add to your summer playlists. From the return of indie sleaze icons The Strokes, who are set to release their first album in six years, "Reality Awaits", to a British pop star hoping to recreate the success of her 2024 record Brat – the album that sparked the viral internet trend dubbed "Brat Summer". Her seventh album, Music, Fashion, Film, features musician John Cale, designer Marc Jacobs and filmmaker Martin Scorsese on the cover. Here's the single "Wink Wink".

items.title: Senator Lindsey Graham died of aortic tear, examiner says
items.description: Graham, a close Republican ally of Donald Trump, died at the age of 71 on Saturday evening.
